gary_2026_06_16_ai_security_battleground.title: AI Security Battleground - Threats & Defenses
gary_2026_06_16_ai_security_battleground.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_06_16_ai_security_battleground.summary: Gary's Guide: AI is creating entirely new attack surfaces - from prompt injection & data poisoning to autonomous agent misbehavior & system-level exploits. The AI Security Battleground brings together security leaders, red-teamers, & AI architects to map out the threats emerging as AI systems become more powerful & more embedded across the enterprise. Expect practical insights, hard-won lessons, & a clear view of the defenses required to secure the next era of AI deployments. Want to stay ahead of the threats targeting modern AI systems? Join the security, red-teaming, & AI leaders redefining defense strategy.

gary_2026_06_16_voice_agents_workshop_no.title: Voice Agents Workshop - No Coding Skills
gary_2026_06_16_voice_agents_workshop_no.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_06_16_voice_agents_workshop_no.summary: Gary's Guide: Let's build Voice & Text conversational agents in an evening - connect them with data, & deploy on Phone, Web, or WhatsApp. Agents that keep a memory of your customers/users, answer questions, solve their problems, & hand off to a human when things get complex. This is a hands-on workshop to build agents. You don't need prior coding experience but you need to be familiar with Claude & have a pro subscription. Agenda: a. Learn the basics of Agent Building - like Context, memory, RAG. b. Connect your agent to your own data & knowledge base c. Add memory so your agent remembers past conversations for each customer or user separately. d. Clone your own voice - to use in the agent, or pick voices from different providers like Elevenlabs, Microsoft Azure, Cartesia. e. Deploy your agent live on Phone, Web, or WhatsApp f. Learn how to test your agents for hallucinations, or inaccuracies g. Set up smart human hand-off for tricky cases h. Networking over pizza & drinks Who it's for: Founders, product managers folks, support teams, & anyone curious about AI - whether or not you write code. What you'll leave with: A working conversational demo agent you built yourself, ready to share & improve upon. $100 in platform credits from Chanl team to continue building & improving your agents. Have questions? Join our WhatsApp group for questions & staying in touch for future events https://chat.whatsapp.com/GmYnOkJqKQB0xOs5P46NO2

gary_2026_06_16_inference_in_the_clouds_.title: Inference In The Clouds - Rooftop Happy Hour
gary_2026_06_16_inference_in_the_clouds_.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_06_16_inference_in_the_clouds_.summary: Gary's Guide: Join us above the city for an evening of drinks, views, & real talk on AI. Baseten is hosting a rooftop happy hour during AWS Summit for ML engineers, AI founders, & technical leaders building on modern inference stacks. Whether you're scaling production workloads, exploring open-source models, or figuring out what "cost-efficient inference" actually looks like in practice, this is a good room to be in. Come for the cocktails. Stay for the conversations. About Baseten Baseten - the lightning-fast, highly reliable, & massively scalable production inference platform. In the inference world, speed = money, & Baseten is the fastest inference platform out there. See how Zed & Amp are making every millisecond count.

gary_2026_06_17_amazon_aws_summit.title: Amazon AWS Summit
gary_2026_06_17_amazon_aws_summit.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_06_17_amazon_aws_summit.summary: Gary's Guide: Experience AWS Summit New York City AWS Summit New York City is a free one-day event where you can get hands-on with the latest in cloud & AI innovation. From agentic AI to serverless computing, discover the technologies that are changing industries & empowering businesses to lead in the digital era. This event is your opportunity to engage with industry leaders, collaborate with peers, & get the answers to your questions directly from AWS experts. Customize your experience by choosing from a variety of sessions that best fit your business needs, including interactive workshops & customer showcases, all while networking with like-minded professionals. Event highlights 200+ Sessions Explore expert-led sessions across agentic AI, data, security & cloud innovation, plus walk-in labs, hands-on workshops, & live demos designed to help you learn, experiment & innovate. AWS Training & Certification Enhance your AWS Cloud expertise through hands-on learning, challenges, & expert-led training. 8-bit Agents Activation Build & deploy AI agents in minutes-watch your custom 8-bit sprite solve challenges autonomously. AWS Sports Zone Discover AI innovation through two demos: Fuball for All & NBA PlayFinder, using AWS cloud & agentic AI to deliver performance insights & enable instant searches of play sequences. AWS for Industries Zone Discover tailored solutions for your sector's unique challenges with industry experts. What to expect During this free one-day event, you will learn more about AWS through keynotes, hands-on technical sessions, & interactive learning opportunities that cover a range of topics, from cloud migration to AI. Engage with your peers to learn how they have leveraged cloud & AI technology to transform their businesses, applying the latest innovations from the AWS community to tackle your toughest use cases. No matter your knowledge level, come learn something new at the AWS Summit New York City.

gary_2026_06_17_table_solns_for_founders.title: Table - Solns For Founders

gary_2026_06_17_table_solns_for_founders.summary: Gary's Guide: Bring your toughest startup challenge, leave with next steps. Table is a community of 1,300+ founders, operators, advisors, & investors. In each session, participants surface real challenges, vote on what matters most, & engage in focused, fast-paced discussions to unlock practical solutions. In a single morning, you'll gain actionable insights, learn new perspectives, & meet peers also looking to move forward. Table is sponsored by Foley Hoag LLP. Foley Hoag helps founders navigate every stage of their startup growth.

gary_2026_06_17_ai_philanthropy_strategi.title: AI & Philanthropy - Strategic Leadership Forum
gary_2026_06_17_ai_philanthropy_strategi.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_06_17_ai_philanthropy_strategi.summary: Gary's Guide: AI & Philanthropy - A Strategic Leadership Forum. The Critical Dialogue: Moving from AI Anxiety to Actionable Strategy The rapid rise of Artificial Intelligence presents a foundational moment for philanthropy & for civil society. AI is no longer a future consideration - it is actively reshaping how nonprofits deliver services, how foundations evaluate impact, & how both make decisions about resource allocation. And yet many organizations are paralyzed by the hype surrounding AI, unsure of how to invest, implement, & govern the technology responsibly. This roundtable (Chatham House Rule) will convene a small group of senior foundation & nonprofit leaders for a candid dialogue about what AI adoption actually looks like at the leadership level: where it's working, where the governance gaps are real, & how funders & implementers can move in closer alignment. This gathering is being organized by All Tech Is Human & Forum One. Please let us know about your interest & ideas below. Please note: because of the highly curated nature of this gathering, we cannot accommodate everyone who expresses interest. This gathering will take place on Thursday, June 17th from 3-7pm. The roundtable will be hosted by Idealist (located in midtown Manhattan) from 3:00 to 5:30pm, followed by a nearby reception. In addition, this roundtable will be building off the learnings of a similar gathering we held in Washington, DC in April that has participation from the Gates Foundation, Omidyar Network, IREX, Porticus, Bloomberg Philanthropies, NetHope, Project Evident, Annie E. Casey Foundation, & more.

gary_2026_06_18_craft_code.title: Craft & Code
gary_2026_06_18_craft_code.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_06_18_craft_code.summary: Gary's Guide: We're starting a series of events at the [untitled] office & this is the first one. We want to get the most craft & design obsessed engineers & product people in New York into one room. The ones who treat design as part of the work rather than a layer on top of it, & who can tell the difference between something built well & something just shipped. [untitled] is built on the same belief about music. Most of the tools musicians use were made without much care, & we think that is worth fixing. The people who understand why that matters tend to be the same people who understand it in software. For this first event we're co-hosting with one of the most craft-obsessed teams in software. We'll share who soon. The night itself: A fireside with a few engineers talking honestly about craft, taste, & how they actually work. DJs before & after, drinks, & enough time to meet everyone else in the room. Thursday, June 18th. Doors at 6. Space is limited, so RSVP early.

gary_2026_06_18_ai_engineers_poker_game_.title: AI Engineers - Poker & Game Night
gary_2026_06_18_ai_engineers_poker_game_.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_06_18_ai_engineers_poker_game_.summary: Gary's Guide: Join us for a private, invite-only Poker & Game Night for founders, engineers, & VCs building the next generation of AI companies. We'll have four poker tables running throughout the night, plus board games & video games including Smash Bros & Mario Kart. Complimentary drinks & pizza are on us. High signal, low pressure. Come play poker, jump into games, & spend the night with some of NYC's top engineers, founders, & operators. Limited seats. Registration: require linkedin, company, job title, & include the following multiple choice (single select) How would you like to participate? Poker has a $100 buy-in & limited seating. For guests who select poker, seats are first come, first served at check-in. *Guests accepted into Fonzi Match Day will be guaranteed a poker seat. To join Match Day, visit Fonzi, click Get Hired, & apply. Options: Poker ($100 buy-in, seat not guaranteed) Hang Out & Other Games Hiring? Want intros to startups?

gary_2026_06_18_founder_x_investor_mini_.title: Founder x Investor Mini Golf
gary_2026_06_18_founder_x_investor_mini_.label: Startup / Business
gary_2026_06_18_founder_x_investor_mini_.summary: Gary's Guide: Trade the formal networking mixers for a little friendly competition. Join founders Rob Guthy & Stephen Michael for founders, investors, operators, & startup enthusiasts for an evening of mini golf, meaningful conversations, & new connections. Whether you're actively raising capital, looking for your next investment opportunity, exploring a new venture, or simply hoping to expand your network, this event creates a relaxed environment where relationships can develop naturally. Between putting challenges & laughs over missed shots, you'll have the chance to connect with ambitious people who are building, investing, & shaping the future of innovation. No pitches required just genuine conversations & a willingness to have fun. Explore Catalyst's concierge services including direct investor intros, pitch deck revisions, profile optimization, spotlight features, & VIP access https://catalystintro.com/concierge View the Catalyst Intro deck Catalystintro.com/app Book time with us directly https://calendar.app.google/ekond6f4NQY3dPvZ9

gary_2026_06_23_the_shortlist_founder_sh.title: The Shortlist - Founder Showcase
gary_2026_06_23_the_shortlist_founder_sh.label: Startup / Business
gary_2026_06_23_the_shortlist_founder_sh.summary: Gary's Guide: The Shortlist NYC - June Founder Showcase (#6) The Shortlist is back for our monthly Founder Showcase & the room keeps getting better. This is a monthly curated, application-only evening connecting early-stage founders with exceptional operators across New York. No panels. No pitches to VCs. Just six founders building breakout companies, a room full of people who've built things themselves, & the kind of candid conversation you don't get at most events. Each founder will share what they're building, why it matters, & the specific roles they're actively hiring for right now - followed by a mixer. What to expect: Founders presenting at the seed through Series B stage. Operators, builders, & executives who've seen what good looks like. An intentionally small room designed for real connection. Who this is for: Exceptional operators - whether you're actively exploring your next role, casually curious, or simply want to stay close to what's being built in NYC right now. Founders will be announced in the days leading up to the event. Attendance is intentionally limited. Apply to be considered. More information on The Shortlist can be found here. Founded by Andrew Yeung & Ivor Straford.

gary_2026_06_24_nyc_ai_demos.summary: Gary's Guide: NYC AI Demos returns to The Refinery at Domino on June 24 for its tenth installment for one more can't-miss evening before we break for summer. This special edition features a hand-picked lineup of fast-growing startups selected by some of New York's most influential venture firms: Thrive Capital, First Round Capital, Index Ventures, Inspired Capital, Able Partners, & more - firms that collectively manage more than $60 billion in assets & have backed some of the defining companies of the last decade. As always, expect live demos, sharp conversations, & a room full of founders, investors, engineers, & operators from across New York's tech ecosystem. Space is limited.

gary_2026_06_25_product_summit.title: Product Summit
gary_2026_06_25_product_summit.label: Startup / Business
gary_2026_06_25_product_summit.summary: Gary's Guide: A one-day summit for product leaders & professionals at the edge of innovation. Themes being explored: Leading Product Strategy Through Disruption Frameworks for navigating market uncertainty, maintaining cross-functional alignment, & building resilient product roadmaps in rapidly evolving industries. Building Trust in AI-Driven Products Best practices for integrating intelligent systems & autonomous features while prioritizing user confidence, data privacy, & transparent user experiences. Unlocking Product-Led Growth & Retention How to build self-sustaining ecosystems by combining continuous user discovery, community engagement, & operational discipline to drive enduring product growth. "Shifting Left" in the Product Lifecycle Practical frameworks for accelerating time-to-value & reducing risk by bringing continuous discovery, technical feasibility, & quality assurance to the earliest stages of development. The Product Manager of the Future How autonomous agents, large language models, & advanced tooling are reshaping product workflows, strategic decision-making, & the PM's role in human-computer interaction.

gary_2026_06_26_ai_engineers_game_night.title: AI Engineers - Game Night
gary_2026_06_26_ai_engineers_game_night.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_06_26_ai_engineers_game_night.summary: Gary's Guide: Game Night with NYC's Top EngineersnWe're bringing together some of the best engineers in New York for a night of Smash Bros, Mario Kart, board games, & good conversation. Drinks & pizza are on us.nnWhat is this?nA game night for engineers & builders in NYC's tech ecosystem.nNo pitches or networking vibes - were just hanging out.nnWho's coming?nA curated mix of engineers from tier 1, VC-backed startups across AI/ML, infrastructure, & full-stack product. Seed to Series C+.nnAbout the host:nFonzi is a recruiting platform focused exclusively on product & AI engineering hires in NYC & SF. We work with 75+ open roles across the best startups in the city.nnHiring?nWant intros to startups?nnSpots are limited.

gary_2026_07_11_founder_led_growth_the_c.title: Founder-Led Growth - The Content System That Brings Customers
gary_2026_07_11_founder_led_growth_the_c.label: Startup / Business
gary_2026_07_11_founder_led_growth_the_c.summary: Gary's Guide: Agendan1:302:00 PM Arrival & Check-InnDoors open at 1:30 PM. Guests can arrive, settle in, & meet others before the first seminar begins. Early guests may also wait at the venue for the second session.n2:002:30 PM Seminar Session 1nA 30-minute seminar covering the main framework, examples, & takeaways.n2:302:45 PM Q&A for Session 1nAudience questions & discussion.n2:453:00 PM Intermission / Open ConversationnA short transition window before the second session begins.n3:003:30 PM Seminar Session 2nA repeat of the same 30-minute seminar for guests who prefer the later session.n3:303:45 PM Q&A for Session 2nAudience questions & discussion.n3:454:00 PM Open ConversationnGuests are welcome to stay after the seminars for casual conversation, follow-up questions, & deeper discussion.nNote: Seminar Session 2 is a repeat of Seminar Session 1. Guests only need to attend one session.nMost founders are told to post more content. That advice is expensive, vague, & usually wrong. If you're trying to grow a real business, content should not be a popularity contest, it should be a customer acquisition systemnThis seminar is a practical, direct look at how founders can use content to attract buyers, start conversations, & turn attention into revenue. Jun will walk through the core framework behind content that actually works, the kind that earns trust, creates demand, & brings in customers without wasting weeks guessing what to say nextnnThis session was widely well-received during New York Tech Week, & the response made one thing clear, founders are hungry for a better way to think about content. The ideas shared there helped cut through a lot of noise, & this time the conversation goes even deeper. Expect sharper examples, more usable tactics, & a clearer picture of how to build a content engine around growth, not vanitynnIf you've been treating content as a side task, a branding exercise, or a chore to keep up with, this event will challenge that thinking. The goal is simple, show you how to turn founder-led content into a repeatable system that supports sales & opens doorsnCommunity PartnernnSupermoonis a global network of 42,000 founders, builders, & investors, with over 200 in-person events hosted worldwide.nSupermoon operates Growth Engine, a NYC-based startup program supporting early-stage founders across deep tech, AI, fintech, & digital assets.

gary_2026_07_14_understanding_work_agenc.title: Understanding Work, Agency & Stewardship In Age Of Generative & Agentic AI
gary_2026_07_14_understanding_work_agenc.label: Tech / AI
gary_2026_07_14_understanding_work_agenc.summary: Gary's Guide: All Tech Is Human (ATIH), in partnership with the Notre Dame-IBM Tech Ethics Lab, will host a two-part workshop series focused on perceptions, adoption, & experience with Agentic AI systems across the workforce. Led by Jen Weedon, ATIH Braintrust member, this series aims to surface both strategic & lived insights from across organizational levels, generating a more holistic set of data points to inform responsible research & practice in the enterprise space. On Tuesday, July 14, the conversation moves toward organizational leadership & governance. We will bring together 20-30 senior executives who are currently standing at the crossroads of automation. Whether you are navigating the complexities of adoption, facing regulatory bottlenecks, dealing with internal reorgs or downsizing, and/or weighing the decision to automate core processes, this executive roundtable offers a high-level forum to discuss the ethics of efficiency & the push towards AI-first or AI-native transformations. This workshop aims to surface the process, knowledge, tensions, & skill gaps. Together, we will explore how leadership can drive innovation while maintaining the trust & integrity essential to the modern enterprise. Express your interest below. This workshop will be four hours long. The workshop series will explore how different types of organizations are approaching Agentic AI - from those whose systems are already deployed, to those preparing for rollout, to those still in exploratory phases. By comparing lived experiences with Agentic AI to expectations across roles & organizational contexts, the workshops will surface concerns, decision-making realities, & unintended consequences. The goal is to understand better the dynamics shaping acute & sustained adoption, governance, & the cascading effects on the workforce. Day 2: Agentic AI from the Leadership Perspective (Organizational & Ecosystem Leaders) Agenda forthcoming

jc_culture_10003555_2026_06_17_hamilton_.label: Festival / Market
jc_culture_10003555_2026_06_17_hamilton_.place: Hamilton Park, 9 McWilliams Pl., Jersey City, NJ
jc_culture_10003555_2026_06_17_hamilton_.summary: Join us at the Hamilton Park Farmers Market, your midweek destination for fresh, local, and thoughtfully crafted food and goods. Each week, we’re proud to feature farm-fresh produce from Stony Hill Farm alongside a curated selection of specialty vendors. Browse a variety of offerings including our long time vendor Pickles and Olives, fresh seafood from Scale Fish Market, and prepared foods perfect for dinner. You can also find baked goods, candles, personal care products, and more. You can even take advantage of convenient services like on-site knife sharpening while you shop. On the first and second Wednesdays of each month, enjoy live entertainment that adds to the vibrant, community atmosphere of the market. Whether you’re shopping for the week, grabbing a bite, or simply enjoying the park, the Hamilton Park Farmers Market is a welcoming space to connect with local vendors and neighbors.

jc_culture_38567_2026_06_20_life_screeni.title: Life Screening Men’s Prostate and Colon Screening
jc_culture_38567_2026_06_20_life_screeni.label: Music / Movies
jc_culture_38567_2026_06_20_life_screeni.place: Lincoln Park, 1 County Rd 605, Jersey City, NJ
jc_culture_38567_2026_06_20_life_screeni.summary: Father’s Day is around the corner, and there’s no better gift than health. Another Glamming The Warrior (GTW) initiative is bringing you Life Screening, an event dedicated to encouraging men to prioritize their health through prostate and colon cancer screenings at Lincoln Park in Jersey City. In collaboration with @rwjbarnabas_health and @hfp_nj, our mission is to screen 200 men and continue saving lives through early detection. Held the day before Father’s Day, this all-men’s event is a reminder for men to take care of themselves, not only for their own future, but for the families who love and depend on them. Bring your father, husband, brother, son, uncle, friend, or any man in your life and encourage them to take this important step. Pre-registration is required. Register the men you love today. This is more than an event, it’s a movement to normalize men’s health and save lives through early detection. #LifeScreening #GlammingTheWarrior #MensHealthMatters #FathersDay #JerseyCity

jc_culture_39997_2026_06_27_playwriting_.title: Playwriting Workshop for the History and Preservation Community
jc_culture_39997_2026_06_27_playwriting_.label: Art / Culture
jc_culture_39997_2026_06_27_playwriting_.place: Museum of Jersey City History, 298 Academy St., Jersey City, NJ
jc_culture_39997_2026_06_27_playwriting_.summary: This workshop will encourage intersectionality and investigation as a way to write new plays based on aspects of history and preservation. Have you ever wanted to write a play? Have you found a niche piece of history that excites you? Do you want a space where you can write and talk about your favorite facts? Join M. D. Schaffer, playwright of “Drapetomania: A Negro Carol,” in a workshop that looks to give attendees the skills and structures to make art inspired by their historical interests. This is a collaboration between the Garden State New Play Festival, Jersey City Landmarks Conservancy and Museum of Jersey City History.

jc_culture_39719_2026_07_04_tirai_a_solo.title: Tirai – A Solo Bharatanatyam Showcase | July 4th, Jersey City
jc_culture_39719_2026_07_04_tirai_a_solo.label: Art / Culture
jc_culture_39719_2026_07_04_tirai_a_solo.place: ART HOUSE, 345 MARIN BLVD, JERSEY CITY
jc_culture_39719_2026_07_04_tirai_a_solo.summary: Bharatanatyam in its most intimate form – one dancer, live Carnatic music, and the eternal question of what lies beyond the veil. Tirai is a solo Bharatanatyam showcase performed by Sneha, a dancer and teacher with nearly 30 years of practice, accompanied by a live Carnatic ensemble. The performance traces the soul’s journey through invocation, surrender, and the dissolution of illusion – weaving together classical movement, rhythm, and storytelling in an intimate black box setting. Bharatanatyam is a classical Indian dance tradition rooted in centuries of art, music, and devotion, and Tirai brings this living tradition to Jersey City in a deeply personal offering. This show is for anyone who loves the arts, is curious about Indian classical dance, or simply wants to start their Independence Day with something meaningful and beautiful. No prior knowledge of Bharatanatyam is needed – just an open heart.

You can also find baked goods, candles, personal care products, and more. You can even take advantage of convenient services like on-site knife sharpening while you shop. On the first and second Wednesdays of each month, enjoy live entertainment that adds to the vibrant, community atmosphere of the market. Whether you’re shopping for the week, grabbing a bite, or simply enjoying the park, the Hamilton Park Farmers Market is a welcoming space to connect with local vendors and neighbors.

jc_culture_10004094_2026_07_10_the_world.place: Central Avenue, 370-437 Central Ave,Jersey, Jersey City
jc_culture_10004094_2026_07_10_the_world.summary: “The World on Central” will activate one of Jersey City’s most diverse commercial corridors. In partnership with the Central Avenue Special Improvement District (CASID), this event is designed to drive foot traffic, promote local businesses, and position the Jersey City Heights as a regional destination for cultural celebration and economic activity.
